title: hooks-mux: async execution, regex matchers, and per-hook config
dependencyNote: Depends on #637 for async-capable/typed handler support; #637 itself coordinates with #636 missing hook event infrastructure. Implement dependency-ready slices and gate or defer unresolved asyncRewake/runtime integration explicitly.
issueBody: Summary: Async: async:true, asyncRewake:true, once:true. Matchers: regex, OR, if conditional. Config: per-hook timeout, shell selection, statusMessage, disableAllHooks. Env: CLAUDE_ENV_FILE, CLAUDE_EFFORT, CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA. Ref: docs/agent-stack/hooks/missing-capabilities.md sections 3-5, 7

comments.author: tmuskal
comments.body: Depends on: #637 (handler types need async support). Related: #636 (missing events need matcher patterns).

comments.author: a5c-ai
comments.body: Triage confirms hooks-mux lacks matcher evaluation, async execution, per-hook runtime config, and Claude compatibility env propagation. Recommended approach: add failing matcher tests first; extend plan/config types while preserving literal semantics; add runner shell selection/config gates; add async/once/asyncRewake with durable per-session tracking; add Claude env propagation allowlist/precedence tests; wire asyncRewake into agent-platform only after callback/reminder path is defined.

637.title: hooks-mux: add HTTP, MCP tool, prompt, and agent handler types
637.relationship: Issue #639 depends on #637 for async-capable/typed handler support.
637.summary: Extend HandlerRef with a backward-compatible type model, dispatch handlers by type in runner.ts, add HTTP/MCP/prompt/agent handlers, preserve legacy command refs, and coordinate MCP tool support with tool-mux.

636.title: hooks-mux: implement 13 missing hook events (tool, turn, task, session lifecycle)
636.relationship: Issue #636 is related and partly foundational for missing events/matcher patterns; it blocks or informs #637.
636.summary: Reconcile canonical hook event contract across hooks-mux, atlas/catalog data, agent-mux, SDK runtime hooks, and agent-platform dispatch. Runtime emission support must be separated from mapping-only support.

acceptanceChecklist: Reuse-audit findings are rendered before implementation work.
Dependency readiness for #637 and #636 is explicit before implementation.
Runtime call paths are traced from invoke/programmatic engine through plan resolution, runner, merge, propagation, and session persistence.
Literal matcher behavior remains compatible while OR, regex, negation, and if conditions are covered by tests.
Per-hook timeout, shell selection, statusMessage, and disableAllHooks semantics are deterministic and tested.
Async handlers do not block synchronous hook flow, once is tracked per session, and async exits are recorded without orphaned processes.
asyncRewake is wired only if its callback/reminder path is defined; otherwise it is dependency-gated with explicit tests or documentation.
Claude compatibility env propagation uses an allowlist and tested precedence.
Quality commands pass and final review approves before delivery.
